Based on **FND-SH-004** and **FND-SH-005**, and on the parametric evidence in @fig-failure-boundary and @tbl-max-safe-volume, the recommended path forward for contamination control is **removal or permanent sealing of the bag vent**, with outbound structural margin carried by the existing 0.5 bar circuit relief, **subject to** closure of the items in @sec-closure (especially $V_{fixed}$, bag integrity if the vent is removed, and relief calibration).

The conservative estimate of $V_{fixed} \approx$ {{< var v-fixed-l >}} L is consistent with both the vent-removal recommendation (safe at any volume under PED relief) and the legacy vented disposition (below the {{< var two-psig-return-threshold-l >}} L threshold for the as-built 2 psig valve). Traceable measurement remains required for formal closure but does not alter the recommendation direction.

# Evidence Sources {#sec-evidence-sources}

| Evidence type | Where used in this report |
|:--------------|:--------------------------|
| Confirmed field contamination history | @sec-field-history |
| Closed-form screening thresholds | @sec-thresholds, @tbl-thresholds |
| MATLAB quasi-static cycle model and case tables | @sec-results, @tbl-cases, figures in @sec-actual-mechanism |
| Analytical overpressure bound (ideal gas) | @sec-upper-bound, @eq-dp-bound |
| CoolProp Helmholtz equation-of-state check | @sec-coolprop-verify |
| Parametric sweep / contour simulation (Python, CoolProp) | @sec-parametric-surface, @fig-failure-boundary, @fig-dp-surface, @tbl-max-safe-volume |

# Conclusions {#sec-conclusions}

1. The present 0.5 bar safety relief addresses outbound structural overpressure but does not, by itself, prevent return-leg contamination failure (**FND-SH-001**).
2. The governing failure mode is return-leg underpressure after irreversible nitrogen mass loss during outbound venting; leakage through the bag path is not required to explain it (**FND-SH-001**, **FND-SH-002**).
3. For the {{< var bag-volume-l >}} L bag and stated envelope, ideal-vent screening limits are {{< var no-vent-threshold-l >}} L (no outbound venting) and {{< var ideal-return-threshold-l >}} L (nonnegative return). The conservative estimate of $V_{fixed} \approx$ {{< var v-fixed-l >}} L falls between these thresholds; **FND-SH-003** remains open pending traceable measurement.
4. The existing 0.5 bar circuit relief does not open under physically realizable single-flight cargo temperatures; the relief-opening temperature from @eq-t-relief is infeasible in air freight (**FND-SH-004**).
5. Under @sec-review-assumptions and @sec-validity-conditions, removing the bag vent eliminates the vent-path mass-loss driver analyzed here; parametric results support nonnegative return-leg pressure at PED-level cracking pressure (**FND-SH-005**).
6. The as-built check valve cannot reseat during a single flight cycle: the manufacturer specification requires up to 2 psig reverse differential (**FND-SH-006**), and the model produces less than 2 psig return-leg backpressure across the full operating envelope (**FND-SH-007**). The valve therefore remains open after first cracking (**FND-SH-008**).
7. Multi-leg shipments compound the single-flight mass loss (**FND-SH-009**); the single-flight model underestimates the actual nitrogen deficit (**FND-SH-010**). Handling-driven breathing through the unseated valve provides an additional, pressure-independent exchange pathway (**FND-SH-011**).
8. The overpressure bound holds per-leg regardless of flight count; vent removal remains safe from an overpressure standpoint across multiple legs (**FND-SH-012**).
9. Seal leak rate under elevated gauge pressure is uncharacterized; if the vent is removed, this becomes a potential competing mass-loss pathway requiring a leak-down test (**FND-SH-013**).
10. Alternative check valves do not resolve the fundamental irreversible mass-loss pathway; any outward-only valve retains the failure mechanism (**FND-SH-014**).
11. **Bag-vent removal is a proposed design recommendation**, not an executed hardware change, as stated in @sec-decision-status; formal closure requires @sec-closure.
